title: Linear Transformations
summary: Understand functions that map vectors to vectors while preserving linearity, and represent them as matrices.
content_html:
A linear transformation T is a function from one vector space to another that satisfies:

  1. Additivity: T(u + v) = T(u) + T(v)
  2. Homogeneity: T(ku) = kT(u)

Matrix Representation

Every linear transformation can be represented as a matrix multiplication T(v) = Av.

Common 2D Transformations

TransformationMatrix
Scale by (sx, sy)[sx 0; 0 sy]
Rotation by θ[cos θ −sin θ; sin θ cos θ]
Reflection about x-axis[1 0; 0 -1]
Shear (x-direction)[1 k; 0 1]

Composition

Applying T1 then T2: combined matrix = A₂ × A₁ (note the order — right to left)

Null Space and Range

  • Null space (kernel): all v where T(v) = 0
  • Range (image): all possible outputs T(v)

exercises.prompt: The 2D rotation matrix for angle θ is:
options: [cos θ −sin θ; sin θ cos θ]
[sin θ cos θ; cos θ sin θ]
[cos θ sin θ; −sin θ cos θ]
exercises.answer: [cos θ −sin θ; sin θ cos θ]
